name: Regional Remote Sensing Center-North, NRSC, ISRO - IT
project_details: Evaluation of Hydrologic Model Simulation under a Non-Stationary Climate

raw_row.Station Name: Regional Remote Sensing Center-North, NRSC, ISRO - IT
raw_row.Description: Description: Various regionals-scale studies have shown deterioration in performance of hydrologic models when used under non-stationary climate. However, this observation is yet to be tested on a global scale. Secondly, methods need to be developed to overcome biased performance of hydrologic models under climate non-stationarity. Required Skills & Traits: Python Programming (Numpy, Scipy, Tensorflow, PyTorch, GDAL, NetCDF4)Expected Outcomes: Methodology for calibration of hydrological models for their usage under a non-stationary climate Specific Courses Required: Introduction to Pattern Recognition/ Machine Learning, Machine Learning II/ Deep Learning, Introduction to Python
raw_row.Title: Evaluation of Hydrologic Model Simulation under a Non-Stationary Climate

name: SMARTSA TECH PRIVATE LIMITED-Onsite
project_details: -,Description:This project focuses on building a personalized, multimodal agentic AI system capable ofresolving student doubts through voice, text, and image inputs. The AI agent contextuallyunderstands questions across subjects and responds with step-by-step explanations in aconversational, human-like manner. The system integrates with WhatsApp Business API toenable autonomous doubt resolution directly on the platform students already use.Additionally, a native Android application is developed to provide a seamless mobileexperience. The project emphasizes real-time inference, multimodal understanding, andautonomous conversation management without human escalation for standard queries.Skill sets:- Multimodal AI &amp; Speech Processing: Handling text, voice, and image-based doubtinputs using LLMs and vision models- Agentic Conversation Design: Building autonomous, context-aware dialogueagents that maintain session history- WhatsApp Business API Integration: Enabling automated doubt solving throughWhatsApp using the Meta Cloud API- Android App Development: Building a mobile application for doubt submission,history, and AI interaction- Personalization &amp; Adaptive Response: Tailoring explanations to the user&#39;s gradelevel, learning pace, and prior contextExpected learning:- Gaining hands-on experience in multimodal AI system design combining voice,text, and image understanding- Implementing WhatsApp API-based autonomous workflows for real-worldstudent interaction- Developing a production-grade Android application integrated with an AI backend- Designing personalized conversational agents that adapt to individual studentneeds- Understanding real-time inference pipelines for low-latency doubt resolutionSpecific courses required for project execution:- Natural Language Processing &amp; Conversational AI (for dialogue management andresponse generation)- Computer Vision &amp; Multimodal Learning (for image-based doubt interpretation)- Mobile Application Development – Android (for building the student-facing app)- Software Engineering &amp; API Integration,Description:This project involves building an agentic AI system that functions as a Subject MatterExpert (SME) in K12 mathematics, capable of autonomously generating high-quality,curriculum-aligned practice content at scale. The system is designed to produce over10,000 pages of structured mathematics practice material spanning Grades 1 through 12,covering topics from foundational arithmetic to advanced algebra, geometry, and calculus.The generated content is deployed to a production environment accessible to studentsand educators. A web-based admin panel is developed for content review, quality control,topic configuration, and deployment management. The project emphasizes promptengineering, content quality assurance pipelines, and scalable AI deployment.Skill sets:- Agentic AI &amp; Prompt Engineering: Designing LLM-based agents with structuredprompting for consistent, accurate mathematical content generation- Curriculum Design &amp; K12 Mathematics: Understanding grade-wise learningobjectives and structuring content accordingly- Content Quality Assurance &amp; Evaluation: Implementing automated and human-in-the-loop pipelines to validate mathematical accuracy- Production Deployment &amp; DevOps: Deploying AI-generated content systems toscalable cloud infrastructure- Web Development (Admin Panel): Building a full-stack interface for contentmanagement, review workflows, and deployment controlsExpected learning:- Gaining hands-on experience in large-scale AI content generation using agenticworkflows- Implementing K12 curriculum-aligned mathematics content pipelines fromgeneration to deployment- Developing content quality control systems combining automated validation andstructured review- Building admin-facing web tools for managing, filtering, and deploying AI-generated educational materialSpecific courses required for project execution:- Natural Language Processing &amp; Large Language Models (for content generationand prompt engineering)- Mathematics &amp; Curriculum Studies (for K12-aligned content structuring andaccuracy)- Web Technologies &amp; Full-Stack Development (for admin panel and contentmanagement interface)

The AI agent contextuallyunderstands questions across subjects and responds with step-by-step explanations in aconversational, human-like manner. The system integrates with WhatsApp Business API toenable autonomous doubt resolution directly on the platform students already use.Additionally, a native Android application is developed to provide a seamless mobileexperience. The project emphasizes real-time inference, multimodal understanding, andautonomous conversation management without human escalation for standard queries.Skill sets:- Multimodal AI &amp; Speech Processing: Handling text, voice, and image-based doubtinputs using LLMs and vision models- Agentic Conversation Design: Building autonomous, context-aware dialogueagents that maintain session history- WhatsApp Business API Integration: Enabling automated doubt solving throughWhatsApp using the Meta Cloud API- Android App Development: Building a mobile application for doubt submission,history, and AI interaction- Personalization &amp; Adaptive Response: Tailoring explanations to the user&#39;s gradelevel, learning pace, and prior contextExpected learning:- Gaining hands-on experience in multimodal AI system design combining voice,text, and image understanding- Implementing WhatsApp API-based autonomous workflows for real-worldstudent interaction- Developing a production-grade Android application integrated with an AI backend- Designing personalized conversational agents that adapt to individual studentneeds- Understanding real-time inference pipelines for low-latency doubt resolutionSpecific courses required for project execution:- Natural Language Processing &amp; Conversational AI (for dialogue management andresponse generation)- Computer Vision &amp; Multimodal Learning (for image-based doubt interpretation)- Mobile Application Development – Android (for building the student-facing app)- Software Engineering &amp; API Integration,Description:This project involves building an agentic AI system that functions as a Subject MatterExpert (SME) in K12 mathematics, capable of autonomously generating high-quality,curriculum-aligned practice content at scale. The system is designed to produce over10,000 pages of structured mathematics practice material spanning Grades 1 through 12,covering topics from foundational arithmetic to advanced algebra, geometry, and calculus.The generated content is deployed to a production environment accessible to studentsand educators. A web-based admin panel is developed for content review, quality control,topic configuration, and deployment management. The project emphasizes promptengineering, content quality assurance pipelines, and scalable AI deployment.Skill sets:- Agentic AI &amp; Prompt Engineering: Designing LLM-based agents with structuredprompting for consistent, accurate mathematical content generation- Curriculum Design &amp; K12 Mathematics: Understanding grade-wise learningobjectives and structuring content accordingly- Content Quality Assurance &amp; Evaluation: Implementing automated and human-in-the-loop pipelines to validate mathematical accuracy- Production Deployment &amp; DevOps: Deploying AI-generated content systems toscalable cloud infrastructure- Web Development (Admin Panel): Building a full-stack interface for contentmanagement, review workflows, and deployment controlsExpected learning:- Gaining hands-on experience in large-scale AI content generation using agenticworkflows- Implementing K12 curriculum-aligned mathematics content pipelines fromgeneration to deployment- Developing content quality control systems combining automated validation andstructured review- Building admin-facing web tools for managing, filtering, and deploying AI-generated educational materialSpecific courses required for project execution:- Natural Language Processing &amp; Large Language Models (for content generationand prompt engineering)- Mathematics &amp; Curriculum Studies (for K12-aligned content structuring andaccuracy)- Web Technologies &amp; Full-Stack Development (for admin panel and contentmanagement interface)
raw_row.Title: Details awaited ,Title: Doubt Solver – Multimodal Talking Agentic AI with WhatsApp Integration & Android App,Title: Academic SME Agentic AI – K12 Mathematics Content Generation & Deployment at Scale

name: Siemens Digital Industries Software India Pvt Ltd
project_details:
Enterprise Data Connectors for AI-Driven Engineering Workflows
Description: A multi-agent AI system orchestrates engineering change management workflowsacross Teamcenter. The orchestrator, its agents, and the workflow framework are already built andoperational. However, the system currently has no connectivity to data outside Teamcenter.Engineering decisions routinely depend on information distributed across Teamcenter, SharePoint,and the broader M365 ecosystem. The orchestrator cannot access, correlate, or present this cross-system information today.Skill sets: C#, Basics of Object-Oriented programming, MCP, AgentsExpected learning:Project ScopeBuild the agents, MCP servers, tools, connectors, and data source queries needed to give the existingorchestrator access to Teamcenter and M365 / SharePoint data. The work includes designing andimplementing the agents that reason over cross-system information, the tool servers that exposequery capabilities, the connection and query logic against each data source&#39;s APIs, and anormalization layer that makes structurally different source data semantically consistent for theorchestrator&#39;s agents. All outputs must support multiple rendering formats — text, tables, lists, cards,document previews, relationship graphs, and timelines — so the orchestrator can present resultsappropriately.The components must support both independent invocation against a single data source andcomposed invocation across both sources in a single orchestrated flow.Deliverables1. Document and Draw the features covering agents, tools, data flows, API mappings, and schemadefinitions2. Teamcenter agents, tools, and connectors with at least 20 operational query capabilities3. M365 / SharePoint agents, tools, and connectors with at least 5 operational query capabilities4. Multi-view output system supporting at least 4 distinct rendering formats5. Live integration demonstration6. Test suite covering unit and integration scenariosSpecific courses required for project execution: Object Oriented Programming

name: TCG Centres for Research and Education in Science and Technology (TCG CREST)
project_details:
Qubit control and readout optimization,Development and evaluation of sinale photon sOurce for quantum sensing applications,Quantum Communication Protocols,Basics of Quantum Information,Quantum Error Corrections,The QSVT quantum algorithm,ExploringClassification Models with Kolmogorov Arnold Networks
Title: Qubit control and readout optimizationDescription: Superconducting qubit control and readout is done by driving amplitude modulatedmicrowave pulses. However, depending on pulse shapes, the aubit can experience noise, leading tO iSdecoherence. Thus, certain pulse shapes can be optimized to reduce the decoherence from the drive.Based on basic ideas of filters, one has to generate the pulse shapes by proper sampling of data pointswhich will be driven by the arbitrary waveform generator (AWG) to produce the pulses. One has toprogram the pulses to the control electronics using Python codes.Skill sets: Python programming, Microwave electronicsExpected learning: Generate anmplitude modulated microwave pulses, program pulses to be driven by AWG.Specific courses required for project execution: Python programming, microwave electronics, basic,Title: Title: "Development and evaluation of sinale photon sOurce for quantum sensing applicationsDescription: The project is focused on the setup and evaluation of a single-photon source, a keyComponent in quantum communication, quantum computing, and quantum sensing. A home-bultspontaneous parametric down-conversion (SPDC) setup will be used where a nonlinear crystal will bepumped with a laser to generate correlated photon pairs. One photon acts as a herald, indicating thepresence of its twin. The student will evaluate the source by measuring the second-order correlationfunction g(2)(0) using a Hanbury Brown and Twiss interferometer to confirm single-photon behavior.This hands-on project will provide insight into quantum optics and the practical challenges of buildingreliable quantum light sources.Skill sets: 1. Knowledge of Lab View, Python2. Hands-on experience with LasersExpected learning:Experience in photon detection module1. The student will gain hand-on experience in quantum optics domain2. Advance instrumentation3. Enhance their knowledge of solid-state physics4. Gain experience in state-of-the-art quantum technologies and related accessories 5. Understand the correlation between the theory and experimentsSpecific courses required for project execution: Solid state physics, Quantum mechanics, Nonlinear optics,Title: Quantum Communication ProtocolsDescription: Quantum communication, including quantum cryptography, is future of communicatingboth classical and quantum information. In this project, a student will learn first some notions of quantuminformation to prepare for learning the protocols. After that, the focus will be on some communicationprotocols like Quantum Teleportation, Dense Coding, and Entanglement Swapping. A student will alsolearn some basic quantum cryptographic protocols.Skill sets: The required background is knowledge of quantum mechanics and Titlesome linear algebra.,Title: Basics of Quantum InformationDescription: A student will learn some introductory topics in this area. Some of the topics will include -postulates of quantum mechanics, qubits, no-cloning theorem, gubit gates, generalized measurement,quantum channels, tools of quantum information (partial trace, Schmidt decomposition, purification), GHJW theorem, Naimark theorem, various aspects of quantum entanglement, etc. The topics to becovered will depend on the background of the student.Skill sets: The required background is knowledge of quantum mechanics and some linear algebra.,Title: Quantum Error CorrectionsDescription: Quantum Communication and quantum computation are vulnerable to the noise. IniSnoise is the main limiting factor in carrving out useful quantum computation and communication. VarousStrategies have been suggested to deal with different kind of noises, A student will learn basic strategiesto mitigate the effects of classical and quantum noise One will start with the protocols to deal with noisein class0cal domain and then move on to guantum domain. Basic classical error correcting codes anoquantum error correcting codes will be studied.Skill sets: The required backaround is knowledge of quantum mechanics and some linear algebra,Title: The QSVT quantum algorithmDescription: The student can begin with a reading project on these topics: quantum computing.quantum algorithms, and the QSVT algorithm, and depending on their progress, the student can worKon algorithmic development in the QSVT framework. The proiect duration is 2 months, but if we makesufficient progress, we can continue working on it in online mode (of course this very much depends Onthe student wanting to continue).Skill sets: Prerequisite: linear algebra, and a little bit of quantum mechanics.,Title:ExploringClassification Models with Kolmogorov Arnold NetworksDescription: This project aims to explore the potential of Kernel Analysis Networks (KAN) inthe field of machine learning classification tasks. By integrating kernel methods with deeplearning, KANs offer a promising approach to improve the robustness and accuracy ofclassification models. The project will involve the development and testing of various KANarchitectures to determine their effectiveness in handling different types of datasets, such asimages, text, and audio. Emphasis will be placed on comparing the performance of KANsagainst traditional CNNs and fully connected networks to identify areas of superiority andpotential application.Skill sets: 1) Machine Learning and Deep Learning: Proficiency in building and optimizingmodels using frameworks like TensorFlow, PyTorch, or Keras.i) Statistical Analysis: Strong understanding of statistical methods relevant to performancemetrics and data validation.I) Programming: Expertise in Python, including libraries such as NumPy, SciPy, and scikit learn.) Data Handling: Experience with data preprocessing, cleaning, and transformation techniques for various types of data sources.V) Research and Critical Thinking: Ability to conduct thorough literature reviews and applyinsights to experimental design and problem-solving. Expected learning: In this project onKolmogorov Arnold Networks (KANS), participants can expect to acquire a deep understanding of the mathematical foundations of KANS and their practical applications in machine learning.Throughout the course of the project, learners will develop and refine these advanced models,learning to implement them effectively across various types of data. They will also gain skills inrigorous testing and comparison of model performances, enhancing their ability to conductcomprehensive research and analysis. The project aims to not only improve practical machinelearning implementation skills but also encourage innovative thinking in applying theoreticalknowledge to solve real-world classification problems. Specific courses required for projectexecution: For the successful execution of this project on Kolmogorov Arnold Networks,participants should engage in a series of specialized courses. These include a deep dive intothe theoretical foundations of machine learning, focusing on the mathematical principles thatunderpin models like KANS. Advanced programming courses in Python tailored towardsmachine learning applications will be crucial for developing and implementing complexalgorithms effectively. Courses in data science methodologies will equip researchers with thenecessary skils to manage, preprocess, and visualize data, preparing it for sophisticatedanalysis. Additionally, a strong background in statistical analysis is essential for validating andinterpreting model performances accurately. Finally, staying updated with emergingtechnologies in artificial inteligence through specialized courses will provide insights intoinnovative approaches and cutting-edge developments in the field, facilitating the creativeapplication of KANs in diverse classification tasks.

raw_row.Station Name: TCG Centres for Research and Education in Science and Technology (TCG CREST)
raw_row.Description: Title: Qubit control and readout optimizationDescription: Superconducting qubit control and readout is done by driving amplitude modulatedmicrowave pulses. However, depending on pulse shapes, the aubit can experience noise, leading tO iSdecoherence. Thus, certain pulse shapes can be optimized to reduce the decoherence from the drive.Based on basic ideas of filters, one has to generate the pulse shapes by proper sampling of data pointswhich will be driven by the arbitrary waveform generator (AWG) to produce the pulses. One has toprogram the pulses to the control electronics using Python codes.Skill sets: Python programming, Microwave electronicsExpected learning: Generate anmplitude modulated microwave pulses, program pulses to be driven by AWG.Specific courses required for project execution: Python programming, microwave electronics, basic,Title: Title: "Development and evaluation of sinale photon sOurce for quantum sensing applicationsDescription: The project is focused on the setup and evaluation of a single-photon source, a keyComponent in quantum communication, quantum computing, and quantum sensing. A home-bultspontaneous parametric down-conversion (SPDC) setup will be used where a nonlinear crystal will bepumped with a laser to generate correlated photon pairs. One photon acts as a herald, indicating thepresence of its twin. The student will evaluate the source by measuring the second-order correlationfunction g(2)(0) using a Hanbury Brown and Twiss interferometer to confirm single-photon behavior.This hands-on project will provide insight into quantum optics and the practical challenges of buildingreliable quantum light sources.Skill sets: 1. Knowledge of Lab View, Python2. Hands-on experience with LasersExpected learning:Experience in photon detection module1. The student will gain hand-on experience in quantum optics domain2. Advance instrumentation3. Enhance their knowledge of solid-state physics4. Gain experience in state-of-the-art quantum technologies and related accessories 5. Understand the correlation between the theory and experimentsSpecific courses required for project execution: Solid state physics, Quantum mechanics, Nonlinear optics,Title: Quantum Communication ProtocolsDescription: Quantum communication, including quantum cryptography, is future of communicatingboth classical and quantum information. In this project, a student will learn first some notions of quantuminformation to prepare for learning the protocols. After that, the focus will be on some communicationprotocols like Quantum Teleportation, Dense Coding, and Entanglement Swapping. A student will alsolearn some basic quantum cryptographic protocols.Skill sets: The required background is knowledge of quantum mechanics and Titlesome linear algebra.,Title: Basics of Quantum InformationDescription: A student will learn some introductory topics in this area. Some of the topics will include -postulates of quantum mechanics, qubits, no-cloning theorem, gubit gates, generalized measurement,quantum channels, tools of quantum information (partial trace, Schmidt decomposition, purification), GHJW theorem, Naimark theorem, various aspects of quantum entanglement, etc. The topics to becovered will depend on the background of the student.Skill sets: The required background is knowledge of quantum mechanics and some linear algebra.,Title: Quantum Error CorrectionsDescription: Quantum Communication and quantum computation are vulnerable to the noise. IniSnoise is the main limiting factor in carrving out useful quantum computation and communication. VarousStrategies have been suggested to deal with different kind of noises, A student will learn basic strategiesto mitigate the effects of classical and quantum noise One will start with the protocols to deal with noisein class0cal domain and then move on to guantum domain. Basic classical error correcting codes anoquantum error correcting codes will be studied.Skill sets: The required backaround is knowledge of quantum mechanics and some linear algebra,Title: The QSVT quantum algorithmDescription: The student can begin with a reading project on these topics: quantum computing.quantum algorithms, and the QSVT algorithm, and depending on their progress, the student can worKon algorithmic development in the QSVT framework. The proiect duration is 2 months, but if we makesufficient progress, we can continue working on it in online mode (of course this very much depends Onthe student wanting to continue).Skill sets: Prerequisite: linear algebra, and a little bit of quantum mechanics.,Title:ExploringClassification Models with Kolmogorov Arnold NetworksDescription: This project aims to explore the potential of Kernel Analysis Networks (KAN) inthe field of machine learning classification tasks. By integrating kernel methods with deeplearning, KANs offer a promising approach to improve the robustness and accuracy ofclassification models. The project will involve the development and testing of various KANarchitectures to determine their effectiveness in handling different types of datasets, such asimages, text, and audio. Emphasis will be placed on comparing the performance of KANsagainst traditional CNNs and fully connected networks to identify areas of superiority andpotential application.Skill sets: 1) Machine Learning and Deep Learning: Proficiency in building and optimizingmodels using frameworks like TensorFlow, PyTorch, or Keras.i) Statistical Analysis: Strong understanding of statistical methods relevant to performancemetrics and data validation.I) Programming: Expertise in Python, including libraries such as NumPy, SciPy, and scikit learn.) Data Handling: Experience with data preprocessing, cleaning, and transformation techniques for various types of data sources.V) Research and Critical Thinking: Ability to conduct thorough literature reviews and applyinsights to experimental design and problem-solving. Expected learning: In this project onKolmogorov Arnold Networks (KANS), participants can expect to acquire a deep understanding of the mathematical foundations of KANS and their practical applications in machine learning.Throughout the course of the project, learners will develop and refine these advanced models,learning to implement them effectively across various types of data. They will also gain skills inrigorous testing and comparison of model performances, enhancing their ability to conductcomprehensive research and analysis. The project aims to not only improve practical machinelearning implementation skills but also encourage innovative thinking in applying theoreticalknowledge to solve real-world classification problems. These include a deep dive intothe theoretical foundations of machine learning, focusing on the mathematical principles thatunderpin models like KANS. Advanced programming courses in Python tailored towardsmachine learning applications will be crucial for developing and implementing complexalgorithms effectively. Courses in data science methodologies will equip researchers with thenecessary skils to manage, preprocess, and visualize data, preparing it for sophisticatedanalysis. Additionally, a strong background in statistical analysis is essential for validating andinterpreting model performances accurately. Finally, staying updated with emergingtechnologies in artificial inteligence through specialized courses will provide insights intoinnovative approaches and cutting-edge developments in the field, facilitating the creativeapplication of KANs in diverse classification tasks.

name: Teach For India
project_details:
Teaching leader in a government/low-income school in Chennai,Communications and Social Media Strategy & Operations leader,Human Resources and Culture leader,Technology towards Organisational Effectiveness leader
Description: Teaching leaders would be directly engaged in teaching a classroom and/or insupporting existing TFI fellows in teaching. The primary role would be support in direct teaching (postplanning a lesson/content) and/or supporting creating resources for existing fellow classrooms. Thiscould be transformational leadership for the students to grow as a leader of an education movementand also show specific skill-sets &amp; project experience for their future career prospects.The person is expected to mandatorily be on site in Chennai during the period of the leadership.Proper onboarding and training would be given. Prior teaching and knowing Tamil are preferencesbut not mandatory criteria.Skill sets: Passion for teaching, classroom management, handling students in a classroom.Previous teaching experience is preferred but not mandatory - as long as you are passionate andopen to learning - the team would support you with planning and execution through this leadership.Expected learning: Teaching is leadership - we see the leader multiple key leadership skillsincluding planning, strategic thinking, setting big goals, executing effectively and resourcemanagement in directly delivering the lesson and also in supporting existing fellows. This can be apowerful learning experience to engage, influence a classroom full of students from underprivilegedbackgrounds.Specific courses required for project execution: No specific course - anyone with interest andpassion for teaching can apply. We will assign grades and content based on interests and strengthsof the student.,Description: Teach for India Chennai is an educational movement in the country, and this personwill play a strategic role in designing and finalizing the communication and social media strategy forthe educational movement in the region as part of this role.This person will also, in addition tostrategizing and designing the comps and the social media strategy for here, operationally some ofthe social media, sharing in our current social media pages, manage the page, enhance the page,and set a long-term operational strategy plan for handling communications in the social media pagefor the region for the next few months.Previous experience in communications and social media handling is preferred but not mandatory.As long as you have the passion to fundamentally revamp and reimagine the social media andcomms strategy, we are good to go.Skill sets: Passion and creativity to reimagine communications, branding and social media for a not-for-profit movement, Canva &amp; other basic designing tools (preferred but not mandatory)Expected learning:If you are someone who&#39;s looking to learn how to design and build an absolutely robust brand for anyorganization, this will be your biggest opportunity to, from scratch, design a communication strategydocument and an operation plan for the entire movement. You will learn with support from mentorswho are experts in communication and social media how to envision a great strategy for comms andsocial media and how to execute it effectively.Specific courses required for project execution:None. Anyone with passion for branding, communication and social media can join.,Description: Teach for India as a movement works with young people, and we constantly want toreimagine what a culture of inspired, passionate, and high-rigor leadership would look like. Thisperson is going to play a pivotal role in helping us design the overall culture strategies, culture rituals,and also how to approach culture in the city for the next few months. They will also be holding forthon making this organization a very attractive organization for future volunteers and leaders to engagewith us over the next few months.Aspect 1: Fellow Wellbeing &amp; EngagementWork closely with the Culture Committee to design and facilitate informal spaces and activities thatsupport fellow wellbeing, connection, and community building.Aspect 2: Culture Rituals &amp; Community CommunicationCoordinate and manage various culture rituals and community touchpoints through WhatsApp,emails, and in-person interactions to ensure consistent engagement across the fellowship.Aspect 3: Volunteer Outreach &amp; Pipeline BuildingReach out to colleges and student communities to build and strengthen a larger volunteer pipeline,including coordinating outreach and maintaining engagement with potential volunteers.Skill sets: Create and maintain organized documents and dashboards that serve as a one-stoprepository for key policies, resources, and program information. Design and create effective youthwell-being and connection strategy and operational plans.Expected learning:This person, this leader, will learn how to go about designing a strong culture of connection, joy, andperformance with a group of adults and young people. Anybody who is aspiring for organizationalleadership, they want to start their own organization, or want to be in HR or culture vertical, this couldbe a great opportunity to be mentored by design and actually create solid projects and structuralplans for the culture of an organization.Specific courses required for project execution: None. Just passion for organisationaldevelopment, leadership development and culture.,Description: Teach for India Chennai currently has a lot of technology processes aroundcommunication, around communication of clarity, technology around how stakeholders interact witheach other, technology around how progress is measured, how progress is captured, and howprocesses are enabled. This person, this leader, and this team are invited to reimagine howtechnology is driving effectiveness in the organization, especially around how clarity is built, howcommunication is more streamlined and effective, and how monitoring and evaluation can becomefar more seamless and easy and powerful in the team.Skill sets: Passion for leveraging technology and monitoring and evaluation towards audacityeffectiveness is the only requirement. Any previous experience with having worked with technologydashboards, technology-centric communications is preferred, but not mandatory.Expected learning:This leader will be directly monitored and mentored by the city director of Teach for India Chennai.They are likely to learn a lot of aspects around project management, lean processes, how to enableprocess effectiveness using technology, and how to enable organization effectiveness usingtechnology. Anybody who is looking for any organizational leadership roles, they want to startsomething on their own, want to be part of the leadership team of great organizations, this is going tobe a lot of learning around how you build an organization&#39;s effectiveness using technology, datamonitoring, and just effectiveness.Specific courses required for project execution: None. Just passion for organisationaleffectiveness, technology enabled efficiency and process effectiveness.

project_details:
Title: Strategic Market Intelligence & Product Commercialization
Description: This project immerses students in decoding industrial markets through advancedcompetitive intelligence and techno-commercial analytics. Participants will evaluate demanddynamics, pricing architectures, and cost structures across key Rajasthan sectors. By integratingproduct lifecycle thinking with business model innovation, students will identify scalablego-to-market strategies and commercialization pathways. The project emphasizes bridgingtechnical feasibility with financial viability, enabling students to uncover high-impact opportunitiesfor industry-academia collaboration. Deliverables will include sector intelligence reports,company-level insights, and actionable product positioning frameworks aligned with real-worldindustrial constraints and growth trajectories.Skill sets: Market research & competitive intelligence Financial analysis & unit economics Business model design GTM strategy formulation Structured problem solvingExpected learning: Understanding industrial market dynamics Translating technology into business valueIdentifying revenue and cost drivers Evaluating commercialization feasibility Building investor-ready insights

Title: Smart Manufacturing & Industry 4.0 Optimization
Description: Focused on transforming traditional manufacturing ecosystems, this project exploresprocess optimization, industrial automation, and sustainable production practices. Students willanalyze shop-floor inefficiencies, material flows, and production bottlenecks using lean andIndustry 4.0 frameworks. Emphasis will be placed on integrating automation, cost engineering, anddesign-for-manufacturing principles to enhance throughput and reduce waste. By engaging withreal industrial use cases, participants will identify opportunities for technology infusion andoperational excellence. The outcome will be a set of high-impact interventions that alignengineering design with economic performance and sustainability imperatives.Skill sets: Process mapping & optimizationLean manufacturing tools Industrial automation concepts Cost & materials engineering Systems thinkingExpected learning: Identifying production inefficiencies Applying Industry 4.0 concepts Linking design with manufacturability Improving operational productivityEvaluating sustainability in manufacturing

name: The Affordable Organic Store
project_details:
Management Trainee
About the RoleWe are looking for enthusiastic and motivated Management Trainees to support dailyoperations across multiple business functions. This role provides hands-on exposure toprocurement, supply chain management, warehouse operations, e-commerce activities, andthe use of AI tools in business processes. The trainee will work closely with different teamsand gain practical experience in the organic retail industry.Key Responsibilities• Coordinate with vendors to ensure timely procurement and smooth follow-ups• Maintain structured procurement records, purchase orders, and supplier documents• Support inbound and outbound supply movement through proper coordination• Monitor inventory levels and stock availability on a regular basis• Assist in improving supply chain flow by reducing delays and operational gaps• Maintain accurate warehouse records and operational reports• Support product listing and updates on e-commerce platforms• Assist in using AI tools for inventory forecasting, stock planning, and reporting• Apply digital tools and systems used in daily operations• Contribute to warehouse cost optimization by improving space utilization, reducinghandling time, and minimizing stock lossesSkills & Qualifications• Basic understanding of supply chain, operations, or e-commerce is an advantage• Willingness to learn and take responsibility• Basic computer knowledge (MS Excel, Word, email)• Interest in organic products and retail operationsWhat You Will Gain• Practical exposure to real-time business operations• Learning experience in procurement, supply chain, warehouse, e-commerce, and AItools• Hands-on industry exposure in an organic retail environment• Certificate and performance-based opportunity for future roles

name: VNR Seeds Pvt Ltd
project_details:
Vision Inspection: AI-Powered Seed Quality & ODV Identification,Seed Traceability & Aggregation
Description:The primary goal of this project is to automate the purity analysis of agricultural seeds. Inthe seed industry, distinguishing between the target seed and Other DistinguishableVarieties (ODV)—seeds that look similar but belong to different varieties—is a critical butlabor-intensive manual task.PS team will develop a vision-based system that uses high-resolution imaging to captureseed samples and apply Deep Learning algorithms to classify them. The system will detectphysical defects, size variations, and subtle morphological differences that define ODVs,ensuring that seed batches meet strict certification standards.Skill Sets:Programming: Proficiency in Python (specifically libraries like NumPy, Pandas, andMatplotlib).Computer Vision: Experience with OpenCV for image preprocessing, filtering, andsegmentation.Machine Learning/Deep Learning: Familiarity with frameworks such as PyTorch orTensorFlow/Keras.Data Handling: Knowledge of data annotation tools (e.g., CVAT, LabelImg, orRoboflow) and dataset balancing techniques.Expected Learning:End-to-End AI Lifecycle: You will gain hands-on experience in the full pipeline—from raw image acquisition and data labeling to model training and evaluation.Model Architecture: Understanding and implementing state-of-the-artarchitectures, such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) or VisionTransformers (ViTs), specifically optimized for small-object detection.Performance Metrics: Learning how to evaluate model success beyond justaccuracy, using Precision-Recall curves, F1-scores, and Confusion Matrices toaccount for the high stakes of seed purity.Optimization: Techniques like transfer learning and hyperparameter tuning toimprove model robustness against varying lighting and seed orientations.Specific Courses Required for Project Execution:To successfully execute this project, foundational knowledge in the following areas ishighly recommended:1. Digital Image Processing (DIP): To understand how to clean and enhance imagedata before it hits the model.2. Machine Learning / Deep Learning: Core theory on neural networks,backpropagation, and loss functions.3. Linear Algebra & Probability: Essential for understanding high-dimensional datatransformations and statistical confidence in predictions.4. Object Detection and Recognition: Specialized coursework or modules focusingon spatial localization within images.,This project focuses on building a "Track and Trace" ecosystem for the agricultural sector.You will be working on the digital backbone that ensures every single seed pouch can beaccounted for from the packing line to the end consumer (Farmer), utilizing high-speedvision systems and hierarchical data structures.Title: TraceabilityDescription: The project involves implementing an end-to-end traceability system on ahigh-speed seed packing line. The core objective is to establish a Parent-Childrelationship across three layers of packaging:1. Primary: Individual seed pouches with unique QR codes.2. Secondary: Bundles or boxes containing a specific set of pouches.3. Tertiary (Shipper): Master cartons containing multiple secondary packs.You will work with Vision Inspection Systems that use AI/ML models to detect anddecode multiple QR codes simultaneously (aggregation), even under challengingconditions like overlapping pouches or high-speed motion. The system validates eachscan against a central database to ensure no duplicates or mismatches occur beforecommissioning the batch for shipment.Skill SetsPython Programming: Proficiency in Python for backend logic, data handling, andintegration with vision libraries.Computer Vision: Experience with OpenCV or similar frameworks for image preprocessingand QR detection.AI/ML Frameworks: Basic understanding of TensorFlow or PyTorch for pouchdetection and segmentation during the aggregation process.HW & NW Integration: * Interfacing with industrial hardware (C-mount cameras,triggers, and PLCs).Network protocols (TCP/IP, MQTT, or HTTP) for communication between theedge camera and the central server.Database Management: Basic SQL (PostgreSQL/MySQL) to manage thehierarchical Parent-Child data tables.Expected LearningIndustrial IoT (IIoT) Workflow: Understand how software interacts with physicalmachinery in a real-time manufacturing environment.Data Aggregation Logic: Learn the complexity of "Parent-Child" mapping where theChild IDs are logically bound to a "Parent" ID in real-time.Edge AI Deployment: How to deploy ML models on edge devices to perform highspeedinference without latency.System Robustness: Developing "Validation Logic" to handle exceptions, such asunreadable codes or missing items in a sequence.Supply Chain Standards: Familiarity with global standards for traceability (likeGS1) often used in logistics.Specific Courses Required for Project ExecutionDigital Image Processing (DIP): Essential for understanding how to manipulateand clean images for better QR recognition.Machine Learning / Deep Learning: To understand the models used for objectdetection (detecting pouches on a conveyor).Industrial Automation / Robotics: To understand the hardware interface and thesynchronization between the conveyor belt and the camera trigger.Database Management Systems (DBMS): To design and query the relationalstructures required for multi-level packaging.Computer Networks: To manage the data flow between the packing line, the localcontroller, and the cloud/enterprise server.

project_details:
Automation of Financial Inclusion Operations,Data Analysis for BC Performance Monitoring
Description: This project involves understanding and improving internal operational workflows used in delivering financial inclusion services. The student will study existing processes, document step-by-step workflows, and identify areas where manual or repetitive tasks can be simplified. Based on this analysis. the student will assist in designing basic automation ideas such as structured templates. slmpla scripts, dashboards, or improved process flows. name: Albatross Energetics
project_details:
Experimental Investigation and Mitigation of Liquid Flow Maldistribution over Vertical Falling Film Surfaces,Experimental Investigation of Adiabatic Regeneration Performance in Liquid Desiccant Systems under Cross-Flow and Counter-Flow Configurations.
Title: Experimental Investigation and Mitigation of Liquid Flow Maldistribution over Vertical Falling Film SurfacesDescription:	In liquid desiccant-based air conditioning systems, uniform liquid distribution over heat and mass transfer surfaces is critical for optimal performance. In the current system, although the liquid is uniformly distributed at the inlet section of a vertical plate, it rapidly converges into streams along the flow path. This maldistribution significantly reduces effective surface wetting, leading to deterioration in heat and mass transfer performance.The objective of this project is to experimentally investigate the flow maldistribution using optical diagnostic techniques and develop strategies to mitigate it. The student will be responsible for commissioning and instrumenting a dedicated test rig, performing controlled experiments across a range of liquid flow rates, and quantitatively characterising the degree of maldistribution. Based on experimental insights, the student will propose, implement, and validate design or operational modifications to improve uniformity and overall system performance.The work will involve systematic experimentation, data acquisition, image processing, analysis, and iterative validation of control techniques.Skill sets:1.      Experimental techniques in thermal and fluid engineering2.      Fundamentals of heat and mass transfer3.      Fluid mechanics and falling film hydrodynamics4.      CAD for rig modifications and distributor redesign5.      Programming (Python) for data analysis and image processing6.      Data acquisition and instrumentation handling7.      Basic optical diagnostics and visualisation techniques8.      Analytical thinking and experimental design methodologyExpected learning:1.      Practical understanding of liquid film hydrodynamics and surface wetting behaviour2.      Hands-on experience in commissioning and operating experimental test rigs3.      Exposure to optical flow visualisation and quantitative image-based analysis4.      Ability to correlate flow distribution with heat and mass transfer performance5.      Experience in iterative engineering design and experimental validation6.      Understanding of how laboratory-scale insights translate into real-world HVAC system performance improvementsSpecific courses required for project execution:1.      Fluid Mechanics2.      Heat Transfer3.      Mass Transfer4.      Engineering Mathematics5.      Basic Programming (preferably Python)6.      Experimental Methods in Mechanical/Chemical Engineering (preferred)  ,Title: Experimental Investigation of Adiabatic Regeneration Performance in Liquid Desiccant Systems under Cross-Flow and Counter-Flow Configurations.Description:	Liquid desiccant systems require continuous regeneration to maintain their moisture absorption capacity and ensure sustained operation in humidity control applications. In adiabatic regeneration mode, the liquid desiccant is directly heated using an external heat source and brought into contact with air to facilitate moisture desorption.The regenerator can be configured in different flow arrangements, primarily cross-flow or counter-flow circuits. Each configuration influences heat and mass transfer characteristics, regeneration effectiveness, thermal efficiency, and overall system performance.	The objective of this project is to evaluate the regeneration performance under different flow configurations experimentally. The student will be involved in commissioning and operating a regeneration test rig, instrumenting the system for temperature, humidity, and flow measurements, and conducting experiments across varying operating conditions (liquid flow rate, air flow rate, inlet temperatures, etc.). The study will compare regeneration efficiency, moisture removal rate, and thermal performance across cross-flow and counter-flow configurations, followed by data-driven analysis and performance benchmarking.Skill sets:1.      Experimental techniques in thermal and fluid engineering2.      Heat and Mass Transfer Fundamentals3.      Thermodynamics of air—water vapour mixtures4.      Psychrometry and humidity measurement techniques5.      Instrumentation and data acquisition systems6.      Programming (Python) for data analysis7.      Basic CFD understanding (preferred but not mandatory)8.      Analytical modelling and performance evaluation9.      Engineering data interpretation and reportingExpected learning:1.      Practical understanding of liquid desiccant regeneration principles2.      Hands-on experience in operating and analysing adiabatic regeneration systems3.      Comparative evaluation of cross-flow vs counter-flow heat and mass exchangers4.      Exposure to psychrometric analysis and performance parameter estimation5.      Development of experimental planning, uncertainty analysis, and validation skills6.      Insight into system-level optimisation of energy-efficient HVAC technologiesSpecific courses required for project execution:1.      Thermodynamics2.      Fluid Mechanics3.      Heat Transfer4.      Mass Transfer5.      Basic HVAC or Refrigeration & Air Conditioning (preferred)6.      Engineering Mathematics7.      Basic Programming (preferably Python)Experimental Methods in Mechanical/Chemical Engineering (preferred)

In the current system, although the liquid is uniformly distributed at the inlet section of a vertical plate, it rapidly converges into streams along the flow path. This maldistribution significantly reduces effective surface wetting, leading to deterioration in heat and mass transfer performance.The objective of this project is to experimentally investigate the flow maldistribution using optical diagnostic techniques and develop strategies to mitigate it. The student will be responsible for commissioning and instrumenting a dedicated test rig, performing controlled experiments across a range of liquid flow rates, and quantitatively characterising the degree of maldistribution. The project includes vulnerability identification, secure nding practices, and development of automated security evaluation tools aligned with industry standards.Sklll sets:      Python / C++ I JavaScript (basic)      Cybersecurity fundamentals       Web technologies (HTML/CSS basics)      Linux environment famlllarlty      SOL        6ecure software lifecyde      Vulnerability acaecsment methodology      Cybersecurity tools usage      Industry security compliance practices      Collaborative software development8Paclttc couraas required for project execution:     Date Structures      Programming Fundamentals     Operating Systems (bash exposure)       Computer Networks   refered,	Description: Students will work on research-oriented implementation of encryption algorithms for secure multimedia and medical image transmission. The project focuses on post-quantum cryptographlc techniques to protect data against future quantum attacks and integrates Al•based anomaly detection to identify unauthorized access, tampering, or abnormal patterns in the system. The work involves mathematical modeling, algorithm implementation, and performance evaluation of encryption and intelligent detection techniques for secure communication systems.  Skill sets Required:•          Python (NumPy, OpenCV, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow I PyTorch)/ MATLAB•          Linear Algebra basics•          Mathematics background•          Algorithm implementation•          Liiboqs / PQCrypto (Post-Quantum Cryptography libraries•          OpenSSL (for encryption implementation)•          Jupiter Notebook / VS Code / PyCharmExpected learning:•          Cryptographic principles•          Encryption algorithm design•          Mathematical modeling in cybersecurity•          Research methodology•          Scientific reporting & presentationSpecific courses required for project execution:•          Discrete Mathematics•          Linear Algebra•          Signals/Mathematics courses•          ProgrammingAI/ML,Description: Students will conduct market research and economic analysis related to cybersecurity and digital security solutions, focusing on sectors such as healthcare, education, finance, and SMEs. name: Bhukhanvala Industries Private Limited
project_details:
Optimization of Boron Carbide (B4C) Abrasive PSD for SiC Wafer Lapping Applications
1. ObjectiveTo establish a clear relationship between particle size distribution (PSD) and cutting performance ofB4C abrasives (240 and 400 grades) on SiC wafers, and to recommend optimized PSD windows for:• Fast material removal• Controlled surface finishing2. ScopeIncluded:• B4C abrasives (240 grit and 400 mesh)• PSD characterization (laser + sieve)• Controlled lapping trials on SiC platesExcluded:• Any change in machine parameters• Any modification to slurry chemistry• Any sensitive or customer-specific processes3. MaterialsAbrasives• B4C 240 grit – Trial 1 (M) and Trial 2 (F)• B4C 400 mesh – Trial 1 (M) and Trial 2 (F)• One additional blend (to be prepared by team)Substrate• SiC plates (standard size for testing)4. Equipment• Sieve shaker (ASTM sieves: 170, 200, 230, 270, 325, 400, 450, 500, 635)• Laser particle size analyzer• Lapping machine (standard production setup)• Weighing balance (precision ±0.01 g)• Stopwatch• Optical inspection (stereo microscope)5. Methodology5.1 PSD CharacterizationFor each sample:• Measure D10, D50, D90 using laser PSD• Conduct sieve analysis and record % retainedPrepare a comparison table:• Laser PSD vs Sieve distribution5.2 Slurry Preparation• Use standard plant slurry formulation• Maintain constant:o solids %o mixing methodo slurry volumeNo variation allowed.5.3 Lapping TrialsFor each sample:Keep constant:• Machine speed• Load / pressure• Time• Slurry feed ratePerform:• Minimum 3 repeat trials per sample5.4 MeasurementsA. Material Removal Rate (MRR)• Measure weight before and after• Calculate:MRR = (Weight loss) / (Time)B. Surface Evaluation• Visual inspection under microscope• Record:o scratch depth (qualitative)o uniformityo presence of deep groovesC. Slurry BehaviourObserve and record:• settling rate• flow behaviour• “cutting feel” (gritty vs smooth)6. Experimental Matrix—exampleSample Type DescriptionS1 240-M Medium distributionS2 240-F Fast cut distributionS3 400-M Medium distributionS4 400-F Fast cut distributionS5 Custom Blend (student designed)7. Data AnalysisPrepare:7.1 PSD vs Performance• D50 vs MRR• D90 vs cutting aggressiveness7.2 Sieve Fraction vs Performance• % coarse fraction vs MRR• % fines vs surface finish8. Expected OutcomesStudents must clearly establish:1. Effect of coarse particles on cutting speed2. Effect of fines on surface finish3. Optimum balance for:o Fast cuttingo Controlled finishing9. Final Deliverables1. PSD Recommendation TablesFor both 240 and 400 grades:• Sieve distribution• D10 / D50 / D90 ranges2. Performance Charts• PSD vs MRR• PSD vs surface quality3. Practical Recommendations• Ideal PSD for SiC wafer cutting• Do’s and Don’ts for abrasive preparation4. Final Report• Maximum 10–12 pages• Data-driven (no theory-heavy content)• Clear conclusions10. Constraints• No deviation from defined test conditions• No access to restricted production areas• All trials to be supervised11. Evaluation CriteriaStudents will be evaluated based on:• Accuracy of data• Repeatability of results• Clarity of conclusions• Practical usefulness of recommendations12. Key Guidance• “More data, less theory”• “If results are not repeatable, they are not valid”• “Focus on what improves cutting — not what looks good on paper”Final NoteThis project is expected to directly support ongoing development work and generate usablespecifications for industrial application.

project_details:
Drawing creation using Al,SmartQC - Machine Learning Enhanced CAD Quality Check Engine
Title: Drawing creation using AlDescription: An agentic Al solution that assists designers in automating 2D drawing creation from 3D CAD models intelligently placing views, dimensions, GD&T and annotations in compliance with customer and industry standards, reducing manual drafting effort significantly.Skill sets: Python, Agentic Al (LangChain/AutoGen), NX Open or SOLIDWORKS API, Basic mechanical drawing interpretation, Prompt engineeringExpected learning: Agentic Al pipeline design, CAD API integration for drawing automation, mechanical drawing standards (ISO/ASME/GD&T), LLM-driven engineering ,Title: SmartQC - Machine Learning Enhanced CAD Quality Check EngineDescription: An ML layer on CitiusKBE's QC-Mate NX Suite that learns from engineer behaviour to suppress false positives, weight violations by context and part type, and continuously improve CAD quality checking accuracy across aerospace, automotive and industrial designs.Skill sets: Python, Machine Learning (XGBoost/scikit-learn), SQL/PostgreSQL, NX Open API, C#, ONNX RuntimeExpected learning: ML model development and deployment, CAD plugin integration, database schema design for QC event logging, Ml-Ops and model versioning

name: KYRO Technologies Inc.
project_details:
Title: KYRO Confidence Index
Description: Construction and Storm Contractors use KYRO for getting paid within 24 hours by connecting with the institutional lenders once their invoices are approved by their respective customers.For the lender to decide if they should offer the line of credit by buying the invoice, they need a confidence index that factors in essential criteria such as the credit rating and history of the debtor who approved the invoice, operational excellence of the contractors etc.,. KYRO needs a Math based model to be built with all the essential factors covered to help the lender decide if the invoice should be bought and the line of credit should be offered. An algorithm will be the output that could be used by KYRO’s AI AGENT for enabling the lender to make the right decision.Skill sets: Applied Mathematics, Data Structures and Algorithms, Artificial Intelligence and Machine LearningExpected learning: Students are expected to learn the depth of net working capital workflow and its significance for Construction and Storm contractors in running their business without any disruption. They learn how KYRO leverages advanced tech such as AI and ML for building a generic solution in this niche, several billion dollars market.Specific courses required for project execution: None

name: PS-I Entrepreneurship track - Okulo Aerospace Private Limited - Non Tech
project_details:
Founder's Office Intern
Founder’s Office (Strategy, Investor Relations, Special Projects), Growth & Operations (Marketing, Sales, Logistics, Process Improvement)About the companyOkulo Aerodpace specializes in the design and development of long endurance UAVs from ground up. The company has developed India's first solar electric UAV as well as first sub 10 kg fixed winged UAV to be certified by the DGCA. The UAV is code named SUVI (payload = 1kg, endurance = up to 8 hours, max operational altitude = 5kms), it has been specially designed for enterprise surveillance. Additionally Okulo is also developing a long ranged tactical UAV (TRL =7) for the defense forces, under the IDEX program. Given it's bigger payload capacity and high operational altitude, it is best suited for border surveillance and strategic locations.

project_details:
Agriculture,Agriculture,Agriculture,Agriculture

Farmers can give voice commands to manage water flow, schedule irrigation, and monitor field conditions, providing hands-free, intuitive, and practical farm management. The system understands local language nuances, making technology accessible to everyday agricultural operations.Skills:  Python & AI/ML, Crop Disease detectionDescription:  An AI-powered system that uses computer vision and deep learning models to detect crop diseases from plant images. name: Swecha - Operating Systems
project_details:
Swecha AI OS,AI-centric Android ROM
Swecha AI OS builds on the legacy of our work with Swecha OS, BalaSwecha, and eSwecha, delivering a next-generation, open-source operating system with AI capabilities at its core. It provides a comprehensive, accessible, and customizable AI Stack, empowering students to learn and experiment with AI, developers to build intelligent applications, and researchers to advance AI innovations. Designed for the broader community, Swecha AI OS encourages collaboration, open knowledge sharing, and socially impactful AI solutions across domains such as agriculture, education, healthcare, and more. By embedding AI at the OS level, it bridges the gap between open-source computing and intelligent automation, while upholding the principles of freedom, transparency, user control, and Technology for Society.,An AI-centric custom Android ROM designed to enhance the mobile user experience through intelligent automation, privacy-focused design, and deep integration of Free and Open Source Software (FOSS).

Description: The project focuses on identifying early warning signals (EWS) that indicate potentialdefault risk in corporate loan portfolios. The intern will analyze financial statements, credit reports,operational indicators, and macroeconomic factors to detect patterns that precede loan defaultsSkill sets: Finance, Excel, Statistics,Description: The intern will analyze whether loan pricing adequately reflects borrower risk and whethersignals of deteriorating financial health are incorporated in pricing decisions. The study aims to identifycases where risk may be mispriced and suggest improvements to risk-based pricing frameworks.Skill sets: Finance, Excel,Description: This project aims to analyze data from the Real Estate Regulatory Authority (RERA) toidentify forward-looking risk indicators for real estate projects financed by lenders. The intern willexamine project registration details, construction progress, delays, developer track record, andcompliance data available under RERA to determine whether these factors can predict financial distressor project delays.Skill sets: Data Analysis, Finance, Real Estate knowledge

project_details:
AI-Powered Candidate Persona Matcher,Visual Engagement Dashboard for Employers,Strategic Partnerships & Ecosystem Development for MyJobFactory
Description: Design and develop a lightweight, rule-based candidate-job matching prototype withinthe Wix platform using Velo (Wix’s developer tool). Instead of full-blown AI, use logic-based matchingalgorithms to connect candidate personas (based on their inputs like interests, skills, experience, andpreferred culture) with company profiles and job postings on MyJobFactory. This prototype willsimulate the &quot;feel&quot; of AI matching while staying within Wix’s architecture.Skill sets:Basic logic-building and JavaScript (for Velo scripting)User flow designUnderstanding of candidate-job fit criteria Basic UI/UX thinking Research &amp; persona mapping Wireframing tools (Figma, Canva, or Wix Editor)Expected learning:How to simulate intelligence in matching without deep AIExperience in product ideation within tech limitationsHands-on exposure to Wix’s developer tools (Velo)Understanding candidate journeys and experience optimizationTranslating user data into actionable logic flowsSpecific courses required for project execution:Product ManagementUX Design / Human-Centered Design Introduction to Programming (JavaScript basics),Description: Design and implement a dynamic dashboard that visually tracks how candidates engagewith employer profiles on MyJobFactory. The dashboard will help employers understand what contentresonates most — such as office tour videos, testimonials, DEI initiatives — and guide them inoptimizing their branding strategies.Skill sets:Data handling using Wix Data CollectionsBasic JavaScript for Wix (using Velo by Wix)Charting libraries (like Chart.js via Wix&#39;s HTMLiframe**)UX design principlesData storytelling and visualizationExpected learning: Hands-on experience with Wix Velo development. Understanding of user engagement metrics in recruitmenttechCreating real-time dashboards using Wix APIs Applying business analytics in a live productenvironmentSpecific courses required for project execution: Data Analytics or Business IntelligenceIntroduction to Web Development or JavaScriptUX/UI DesignMarketing Analytics or Employer Branding (optional),Description: This project focuses on identifying, evaluating, and developing strategic partnerships toscale MyJobFactory&#39;s presence across the recruitment and employer branding ecosystem. Interns willwork on designing partnership frameworks, reaching out to potential collaborators (such as HR techfirms, college TPOs, training platforms, career coaches, and startup incubators), and mapping thevalue proposition of each partnership type. The goal is to create a structured, scalable ecosystem thatdrives mutual value and sustained growth for MyJobFactory.Skill sets: Market research &amp; competitor benchmarking Business communication &amp; partner pitching Strategic thinking &amp; problem solving CRM tools &amp; LinkedIn for lead generation Basic understanding of recruitment / HR tech industryProposal and deck creationExpected learning: Understanding of strategic alliance building in early-stage startups Real-world exposure to B2B business developmentExperience in developing partnership proposals and MoUsBuilding pitch decks, partnership tiers, and co-marketing plansPractical networking and communication skills withindustry professionalsSpecific courses required for project execution:Strategic Management or Business StrategyMarketing / Brand Management Entrepreneurship or New Venture Creation Organizational Behavior / HR Fundamentals Business Communication

name: BigLook - Non Tech
project_details:
Data Ingestion, Feature Extraction & Representation for LMM
Title: Data Ingestion, Feature Extraction & Representation for LMMDescription: Develop scalable algorithms and data pipelines for automated extraction,structuring, and representation of artistic characteristics from large-scale art datasets toprepare training-ready inputs for LMM. The project focuses on building a Data Layer(Ingestion - Structuring - Representation) that processes raw artwork images andmetadata into structured multimodal training data. This includes feature extraction,automated tagging, taxonomy alignment, and embedding generation. The work alsoinvolves implementing fine-grained contrastive learning, multimodal alignment (image +metadata + annotations), and feature fusion strategies to extract artistic attributes suchas style, composition, color palette, texture, period, mood, and technique. The resultingstructured representations will support model training, retrieval systems, reasoninglayers, and agentic workflows. The pipeline will be designed to scale efficiently to50,000+ artworks, ensuring data consistency, quality control, and training readiness forLMM development.Skill sets:Deep learning for computer vision (CNNs, Vision Transformers)Knowledge of multimodal representation learning (image + text + metadata)Experience with transformer architectures and attention mechanismsUnderstanding of contrastive learning and embedding generationKnowledge of feature extraction and visual representation techniquesExperience with large-scale data pipelines and distributed processingFamiliarity with dataset ingestion and preprocessing pipelinesExperience with Python, PyTorch / TensorFlowExpected learning:Building scalable data pipelines for multimodal AI systemsAutomated extraction of artistic characteristicsExperience preparing datasets for LMM trainingExposure to production-level AI data pipeline designSpecific courses required for project execution: Machine Learning, Deep Learning,Computer Vision, Data Mining, Probability and Statistics, Linear Algebra andMathematics III, Object-Oriented Programming, Technical Report Writing, DatabaseSystems, Natural Language Processing

name: BigLook - Tech
project_details:
Art Dataset Curation, Annotation & Preprocessing
Title: Art Dataset Curation, Annotation & PreprocessingDescription: Collect, curate, and structure high-quality multimodal art datasets tailoredfor Cimelium’s domain-specific Large Multimodal Model (LMM). The work involvesbuilding a foundational art knowledge layer using structured metadata, expertannotations, visual taxonomies, and multimodal alignment (image, text, and contextualknowledge). The role includes designing and implementing custom ontology schemas,annotating artworks with visual, aesthetic, historical, and market-related metadata, andapplying advanced data processing techniques such as deduplication, normalization,alignment, and enrichment. The curated datasets will support model training, retrievalsystems, reasoning pipelines, and agentic AI workflows for galleries, auction houses, andart professionals.Skill sets:Dataset curation and annotation for multimodal datasets (image + text + metadata)Data preprocessing, cleaning, and quality control pipelinesExperience with deduplication, normalization, and structured data alignmentKnowledge of art metadata standards (artist, medium, period, provenance, taxonomy)Familiarity with annotation tools (custom annotation pipelines)Understanding of multimodal dataset preparation for LMMsExperience handling large-scale datasets and dataset versioningBasic understanding of embeddings and representation learningProgramming skills (Python, data pipelines, data processing tools)Expected learning:Building domain-specific multimodal datasets for LMMsMultimodal data alignment (image, metadata, expert annotations)Creating training-ready datasets for reasoning and agent-based systemsUnderstanding of dataset optimization for model performance and scalabilitySpecific courses required for project execution: Computer Programming (Python,Data Processing), Linear Algebra and Mathematics III, Machine Learning Fundamentals,Database Systems and Data Management, Technical Report Writing, Introduction toComputer Vision, Natural Language Processing

ESG,Growth,Product Design,Product Management
About the RoleSupport ESG research, data analysis, and reporting for our platform. Gain hands-on experience in sustainability metrics, regulatory frameworks, and ESG benchmarking. Collaborate with product, business, and domain expert teams.Key ResponsibilitiesResearch ESG regulations, standards (GRI, SASB, TCFD), and sustainability frameworks.Collect, clean, and analyze ESG data from corporate reports and databases.Assist in developing ESG scoring models and benchmarking tools.Support creation of ESG reports, dashboards, and client deliverables.Conduct sector-specific sustainability trend analysis and case studies.Help validate ESG data quality and identify data gaps.Participate in client workshops and contribute to thought leadership content.QualificationsCurrently pursuing B.E./B.Tech, BBA, MBA, or related degree from BITS Pilani (any campus).Strong analytical skills and attention to detail.Genuine interest in ESG, sustainability, and climate action.Proficiency in Excel; basic data analysis skills required.Excellent research abilities and report writing skills.Ability to work with cross-functional teams.Preferred SkillsFamiliarity with ESG frameworks (GRI, CDP, Science Based Targets).Experience with data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI).Knowledge of sustainability metrics and carbon accounting.Basic Python/R for data analysis is a plus.Exposure to sustainability coursework or certifications.Understanding of Indian ESG regulations and SEBI BRSR requirements.What You Will GainDeep exposure to ESG domain and sustainability consulting.Hands-on experience with real client ESG datasets and deliverables.Work on platform features that impact enterprise sustainability.Mentorship from ESG domain experts and sustainability leaders.Build specialized portfolio in high-demand ESG field.Network with industry professionals in sustainability space.,About the RoleDrive customer acquisition, activation, and growth experiments for our ESG platform. Work with marketing, product, and sales teams to test growth strategies, analyze campaign performance, and optimize conversion funnels in a data-driven environment.Key ResponsibilitiesExecute multi-channel growth campaigns (LinkedIn, email, webinars, content).Run A/B tests on landing pages, email sequences, and ad creatives.Analyze user acquisition funnels and identify growth bottlenecks.Support lead generation through content syndication and partnerships.Track campaign ROI and create performance dashboards.Research growth opportunities in ESG/sustainability sectors.Assist in building growth playbooks and experiment roadmaps.QualificationsCurrently pursuing B.E./B.Tech, BBA, or related degree from BITS Pilani (any campus).Strong analytical mindset and experimentation skills.Interest in growth marketing, SaaS, and B2B customer acquisition.Proficiency in Excel/Google Sheets for data analysis.Basic understanding of digital marketing channels.Excellent communication and presentation skills.Preferred SkillsExperience with Google Analytics, Mixpanel, or HubSpot.Familiarity with LinkedIn Ads, Google Ads, or email marketing tools (Mailchimp).Knowledge of SQL or basic data analysis (Python/R).Exposure to growth hacking frameworks (AARRR, ICE scoring).Content creation experience (blogs, social media, case studies).Understanding of ESG/sustainability marketing challenges.What You Will GainHands-on growth marketing experience in SaaS environment.Master customer acquisition funnels and growth experimentation.Work on real campaigns impacting platform growth.Learn analytics tools and growth frameworks from experts.Build portfolio of measurable growth experiments.Network with growth professionals in sustainability tech.,About the RoleSupport the design of user-centric ESG platform features. Collaborate with product, engineering, and business teams to deliver pixel-perfect UI/UX for dashboards, reports, and analytics tools in a collaborative SaaS environment.Key ResponsibilitiesCreate wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity designs for new features.Design intuitive dashboards and data visualization components.Conduct user research and usability testing sessions.Collaborate with developers to ensure design fidelity in implementation.Maintain design systems and component libraries.Create user flows, journey maps, and interface guidelines.Support accessibility and responsive design requirements.QualificationsCurrently pursuing B.E./B.Tech, B.Des, or related degree from BITS Pilani (any campus).Proficiency in Figma, Adobe XD, or Sketch.Strong understanding of UI/UX principles and design thinking.Passion for designing data-heavy SaaS applications.Excellent visual design skills and attention to detail.Ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams.Preferred SkillsExperience with data visualization design (charts, graphs, metrics).Knowledge of design systems and component libraries.Familiarity with HTML/CSS and prototyping tools (Framer).Understanding of accessibility standards (WCAG).Exposure to ESG/sustainability domain or enterprise SaaS.Motion design or micro-interaction experience.,About the RoleAssist the product team in developing and enhancing our ESG platform. Gain hands-on experience in product strategy, user research, feature planning, and analytics within a collaborative SaaS environment. Work closely with product, engineering, business, and design teams.Key ResponsibilitiesResearch user needs, market trends, and competitive landscapes in ESG/sustainability.Document product requirements, user stories, and feature specifications.Track development progress with engineering and design teams.Analyze user feedback and product usage data for improvement opportunities.Support product testing, QA, and feature validation.Create product documentation, feature guides, and internal resources.Conduct competitive analysis and benchmarking.Project based engagement will be involved as part of this Internship.QualificationsPursuing or recently completed Bachelor’s/Master’s in Engineering, Management, Sustainability, Business, or related fields.Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.Passion for product development, SaaS platforms, and technology.Excellent communication and documentation abilities.Familiarity with Excel, Notion, Jira, or similar tools (preferred).Preferred SkillsKnowledge of product lifecycle and Agile methodologies.Basic data analysis or product metrics experience.Interest in ESG, sustainability, or climate-tech.What You Will GainPractical exposure to SaaS product development.Work on live features and strategy discussions.Mentorship from seasoned product and tech leaders.Build a strong portfolio in product management.
