name: Celebal Technologies
project_details:
AI-Powered Executive Dashboard & Business Review Automation for the CEO Office,Chief Strategist Office – Market Intelligence & Strategy Operations Framework ,COO Office – Operational Excellence & Delivery Workflow Automation , Investor & Growth Office: Fundraising Ops, Growth Metrics & Investor Reporting Automation.
Title: AI-Powered Executive Dashboard & Business Review Automation for the CEO Office.   Description: Build an automated reporting system that gives the CEO real-time visibility into companywide KPIs : revenue, project health, employee headcount, partner pipeline, and client satisfaction. Collect data from multiple sources (CRM, HRMS, finance sheets), clean and integrate it, and create interactive dashboards with automated refresh. Additionally, build an AI-powered weekly briefing generator that summarizes key business highlights, risks, and action items using LLMs.                                                 Skill Sets: Data collection & cleaning (Python, Pandas), dashboard development (Power BI / Streamlit), API integration, LLM prompt engineering (OpenAI / Azure OpenAI), workflow automation (n8n / Power Automate), basic SQL, business KPI understanding.                Expected Learning: How to integrate data from multiple business systems into a single reporting layer, build interactive dashboards for leadership consumption, use AI/LLMs to auto-generate business summaries, and understand what KPIs matter at the CEO level in a services company.  Specific Courses Required: Python for Data Science Prompt Engineering for LLM Applications SQL for Beginners ,Title: Chief Strategist Office – Market Intelligence & Strategy Operations Framework  Description: Build a structured strategy operations function that automates competitive intelligence gathering, market sizing, and partner/GTM performance tracking. Includes creating repeatable frameworks for evaluating new service lines, geographies, and partnerships : supported by automated data feeds from CRM, finance, and delivery systems. The goal is to move from reactive strategy inputs to a proactive, datadriven strategy cockpit.  Skill Sets: Market research & competitive analysis, data analytics (Python/SQL), CRM analytics (HubSpot/Salesforce), strategic frameworks (Porter's, TAM/SAM/SOM, Ansoff Matrix), workflow automation (n8n, Power Automate), presentation design, financial modeling basics.  Expected Learning: How to build and maintain a competitive intelligence engine, automate market and GTM data pipelines, structure strategy reviews with quantitative rigor, and operationalize strategic planning cycles (quarterly/annual) Specific Courses Required: •      SQL for Business Analysts •      n8n or Power Automate workflow automation Financial Modeling Foundations ,Description: Build an integrated operations command center that tracks project delivery health, resource utilization, bench strength, SLA compliance, and client satisfaction in real time. Automate operational workflows including resource allocation, timesheet aggregation, onboarding/offboarding, and escalation management. Establish SOPs and process playbooks for repeatable operational functions across all delivery units.  Skill Sets: Operations management, process re-engineering (Lean/Six Sigma basics), project management tools (Jira, Monday.com, MS Project), workflow automation (n8n, Power Automate), HRIS/resource management systems, data analytics (SQL, Python), SLA and KPI framework design.  Expected Learning: How to design and implement an operations dashboard for a 2,500+ person services firm, automate resource and delivery workflows, build SOP libraries, and create feedback loops between delivery, HR, and finance functions.  Specific Courses Required: Jira Administration (Optional) n8n or Power Automate for Operations  People Analytics Fundamentals ,Title: Investor & Growth Office: Fundraising Ops, Growth Metrics & Investor Reporting Automation.  Description: Establish a structured investor relations and growth operations function. Automate the creation of investor updates, board decks, and growth metric dashboards (ARR, logo acquisition, retention, NDR, pipeline velocity). Build repeatable models for scenario planning, valuation tracking, and fundraising readiness. Streamline data flows from CRM, finance, and delivery into a single investor-grade reporting layer. Skill Sets: Financial modeling & valuation (DCF, comparable analysis), investor communication, CRM analytics, dashboard design (Power BI / Looker), pitch deck storytelling, SaaS/services metrics (ARR, NDR, LTV, CAC), data integration, presentation design.  Expected Learning: How to build investor-grade reporting from internal systems, design growth metric frameworks for a consulting/AI firm, prepare data rooms and fundraising materials, and automate board reporting cadence. Specific Courses Required: Financial Modeling & Valuation for Startups / Growth Companies SaaS Metrics & Unit Economics Advanced Power BI / Looker for Executive Reporting •      Investor Communication & Pitch Storytelling

name: Heetherark Labs Private Limited - IT
project_details:
UI/UX Design for Heether Smart Vest & Digital Platforms,Title: Data Collection, Exploratory Data Analysis, and Model Building for Posture Analysis,Cloud Infrastructure Development and Management for Posture Analysis
Description: The intern(s) will be involved in designing and improving the user experienceacross multiple platforms, including the Posture Analytics Dashboard, Mobile App UI/UX, andWebsite Optimization. The posture analytics dashboard will require an intuitive and user-friendlyinterface to display real-time insights from the Heether Smart Vest, making it easier for users tounderstand and act on their posture data. The mobile application will be enhanced to ensure asmooth, engaging, and accessible experience. Additionally, the Heether website and landingpages will be redesigned with a focus on improving user navigation, responsiveness, andengagement. Interns will be involved in brainstorming ideas, creating wireframes, developinginteractive prototypes, and testing designs to improve usability. They will work with Figma andAdobe XD to create layouts and user flows while learning how to conduct basic user researchand usability testing. They will also collaborate with developers and product managers tounderstand how design decisions impact the overall product.Skill sets: Basic knowledge of UI/UX design principles, an interest in user research and usabilitytesting, and familiarity with wireframing and prototyping tools like Figma or Adobe XD.Creativity, attention to detail, and a willingness to learn and iterate based on feedback areessential. No prior professional experience is required, but a passion for design and problem-solving is encouraged.Expected learning: Interns will gain practical experience in designing UI UX for a Mobile App,understanding user behavior, and improving digital product experiences. They will learn how toapply design thinking, conduct usability testing, and iterate on their designs based on realfeedback. By the end of the internship, they will have worked on real-world projects that can beadded to their portfolio.Specific courses required for project execution: While prior coursework in UI/UX Design,Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), or Web and Mobile UI Design would be helpful, it is notmandatory.Students with a strong interest in design and a willingness to learn are welcome.,Description: The intern(s) will work on exploratory data analysis (EDA), model development, andoptimization to enhance Heether’s posture analysis system. This project will involve workingwith sensor data from the Heether Smart Vest, understanding patterns, and building machinelearning models to improve posture recognition. Interns will be responsible for datapreprocessing, feature extraction, and tuning models to achieve better accuracy and real-timeperformance.Skill sets: Basic understanding of machine learning concepts, Python programming, and datapreprocessing. Familiarity with EDA, model building, and tuning is beneficial. Strong analyticalthinking and problem-solving skills are key. Prior experience with sensor data or time-seriesdata is a plus but not required.Expected learning: Interns will gain hands-on experience in working with real-world sensordata, building and tuning ML models, and optimizing model performance. They will also developan understanding of data-driven decision-making and AI-powered posture analysis.Specific courses required for project execution: Prior coursework in Machine Learning, DataScience, or Artificial Intelligence would be helpful but is not mandatory. Students with an interestin data analysis, model development, and optimization techniques are encouraged to apply.,Cloud Infrastructure Development and Management for Posture Analysis

name: Indium Software Pvt. Ltd.
project_details:
AI-Powered Symptom Triage Web App [Healthcare],DevLog – AI Sprint Planner & Developer Journal [Software Development / Product Management],BioInsight – AI Lab Report Analyser for Life Sciences [Data Science / Sciences]
Description Build a full-stack web application where users enter symptoms and receive AI-generatedpreliminary assessments, possible conditions, and suggested next steps. The backend uses an LLMAPI (any opensource api (check out open-router)) to parse and respond to symptom input. Adashboard logs user history and flags high-risk patterns.Skill Sets React.js, Node.js/Express, REST API integration, MongoDB, prompt modelling, and LLM basedfunction calling.Expected Learning End-to-end web development pipeline; responsible AI deployment in sensitive domains; promptdesign for structured medical triage; user session managementCourse Involved Foundations of Programming, Data Structures, Introduction to Biology (for context),Description A lightweight project management tool where developers log daily tasks and blockers. An AImodule analyses logs to auto-generate weekly sprint summaries, estimate velocity, suggest taskpriorities using historical patterns, and draft stand-up notes. Includes a Kanban board and teamcollaboration features.Skill Sets React (Kanban with drag-and-drop), Node.js/Express, MongoDB, LLM API any opensource api(check out open-router)), JWT authentication, REST API designExpected Learning Full authentication and multi-user app architecture; building AI features into productivity tools;data modelling for temporal (time-series) task data; product thinking for developer toolingCourse Involved Object-Oriented Programming, Software Engineering, Foundations of Programming,Description Students upload their lab experiment data (CSV/Excel) — titrations, spectrophotometry,microbiology counts, etc. — and the AI analyses the data, generates statistical summaries, detectsoutliers, and produces a structured lab report with natural-language interpretation of results.Skill Sets React, Python Flask, pandas, matplotlib/seaborn, LLM API for report generation any opensource api(check out open-router)), file upload handlingExpected Learning Data ingestion and preprocessing pipelines; automated report generation with AI; scientificcommunication through code; working with tabular data in a web contextCourse Involved General Chemistry / Biology Lab, Probability &amp; Statistics, Foundations of Programming

name: Infogain India Pvt Ltd
project_details:
PS-I 2026 – Project Themes
1. Spec-Driven Development (SDD) for AI-Augmented SDLCOverview: Spec-Driven Development focuses on creating executable specifications that guide AI agents in generating and refining code. It improves reliability, reduces hallucinations, and accelerates development.Key Focus Areas:Design SDD lifecycle integrating NLP-based specificationsBenchmark against Agile and BDDBuild tooling with observability and RBACStudy ethical and energy implicationsDeliverables:Whitepaper and toolkitPrototype integrationsTraining modules and ROI analysis2. Federated Learning for Privacy-Preserving SLM SwarmsOverview: Develop decentralized SLM systems that collaborate across data silos without sharing sensitive data.Key Focus Areas:Federated SLM architecturesCompliance with HIPAA and GDPRIntegration with guardrails and RBACDeliverables:Prototype systemIntegration with cloud storageEvaluation framework3. Adaptive Observability for Multi-Agent SystemsOverview: Create real-time monitoring and evaluation frameworks for AI agents in dynamic environments.Key Focus Areas:Drift, bias, and performance trackingObservability tools integrationEnterprise use cases like supply chainDeliverables:Observability frameworkMonitoring dashboardsROI metrics4. NLP-Driven Semantic Flow Synthesis (Low-Code AI)Overview: Enable non-technical users to build AI workflows using natural language.Key Focus Areas:NLP-based flow generationHR and customer service use casesSecure and ethical prompt designDeliverables:Flow builder extensionBenchmarking resultsDeployment optimization5. Edge-Deployed SLM ConsultantsOverview: Develop lightweight AI agents for edge environments with limited connectivity.Key Focus Areas:On-device AI orchestrationIoT and predictive maintenanceOffline reliability and latency optimizationDeliverables:Deployment guidelinesOptimized edge modelsPrototype system6. Agentic AI Ecosystems for Enterprise OperationsOverview: Build ecosystems of AI agents that automate enterprise workflows.Key Focus Areas:Closed-loop automationIntegration with legacy systemsPredictive maintenance and fraud detectionDeliverables:Integration prototypesAutomation frameworksCase studies7. Self-Healing Agent ArchitecturesOverview: Create AI systems that detect and fix issues automatically.Key Focus Areas:Fault detection and recoveryWorkflow resilienceIntegration with observability systemsDeliverables:Prototype frameworkBenchmark datasetsTraining modules8. Adversarial Robustness in AIoTOverview: Protect AI systems from attacks like model poisoning in IoT environments.Key Focus Areas:Secure training methodsDrift monitoringIndustrial IoT use casesDeliverables:Research paperPrototype defense systemImplementation guidelines9. Zero-Trust Architectures for AIoTOverview: Implement continuous verification systems for secure AI operations.Key Focus Areas:Zero-trust modelsEdge securityRBAC and SSO integrationDeliverables:Framework blueprintPrototype systemBest practices guide10. Ethical AI Frameworks for Bias MitigationOverview: Ensure fairness and transparency in AI systems.Key Focus Areas:Bias detection and mitigationGovernance frameworksHigh-stakes applicationsDeliverables:Ethical frameworkBias evaluation toolkitResearch publications11. Self-Healing Agents for Cloud Cost OptimizationOverview: Use AI agents to automatically manage and reduce cloud costs.Key Focus Areas:Cost anomaly detectionAutomated optimizationMulti-cloud environmentsDeliverables:Open-source frameworkDashboard and analytics toolsImplementation playbook

Description: Develop and enhance the Smart Finder platform (smart-qna.com), an AI-powered intelligent search and question-answering system. Students will work onbuilding a robust Next.js frontend with server-side rendering and incremental staticregeneration, a Python FastAPI backend delivering high-performance asynchronousRESTful APIs, and LLM-powered features including RAG (Retrieval-AugmentedGeneration) pipelines, semantic search, prompt engineering, and fine-tuning usingLoRA techniques. The system will leverage containerized microservices orchestratedvia Docker and Kubernetes, incorporate CI/CD pipelines for automated testing anddeployment, and implement industry best practices such as Redis caching andcentralized logging/monitoring to ensure scalability, security, and optimal userexperience.Skill sets: (i) Proficiency in JavaScript, React.js, Next.js, and Python.(ii) Understanding of LLMs, backend development, CI/CD pipelines, Docker,Kubernetes, and API architecture.Expected learning: Creation of a fully functional AI-powered web application withscalable backend, LLM integration, and production-grade API architecture.Specific courses required for project execution: Web Development, AI/NLP or relatedcoursework,Description: This project involves building and enhancing the Zernyx Hub platform, anintegrated technology marketplace for IP licensing, innovation scouting, andtechnology commercialization. Students will develop features for technologyintelligence, market readiness assessment, TRL (Technology Readiness Level)calculation, and automated patent and IP intelligence including patent search, prior artanalysis, and patent landscape mapping. The platform connects technology discovery,scientific validation, and commercialization readiness, enabling organizations toevaluate feasibility, strengthen IP positioning, and prepare innovations for confidentmarket adoption.Skill sets: (i) Proficiency in modern web development (React/Next.js, Node.js, Python).(ii) Understanding of database design, REST APIs, platform architecture, and NLP/textmining techniques.,Description: This project focuses on developing the Zernyx Labs platform that supportsthe journey from research and validation to product development andcommercialization. Students will build tools for scientific validation workflows, pilotprogram management, experimental data analysis, and structured go/no-go decisionframeworks. The platform serves pharmaceutical, biotechnology, cosmeceutical, andnutraceutical domains, focusing on execution readiness and scalable outcomes acrosslife sciences. Students will also work on data-driven research pipelines and visualizationdashboards for scientific evaluation.Skill sets: (i) Strong Python programming and data analysis skills.(ii) Experience with scientific computing libraries, workflow automation, and datavisualization.Expected learning: Development of a scientific validation platform with structuredresearch workflows, data-driven decision support, and life sciences domain expertise.Specific courses required for project execution: Python Programming, Data Science orrelated coursework,Description: This project involves developing the Civic Info platform that enablescitizens to discover their civic landscape by accessing local, state, and federal civicinformation effortlessly. Students will work on building data aggregation pipelines fromgovernment APIs and public datasets, developing an intuitive search and navigationinterface, implementing geolocation-based civic information delivery, and ensuringaccessibility compliance (ADA/WCAG). The platform aims to make government datatransparent and easily accessible to all citizens.Skill sets: (i) Proficiency in full-stack web development (React/Next.js, Python/Node.js).(ii) Understanding of API integration, data aggregation, and geolocation services.Expected learning: Creation of a civic technology platform that aggregates and presentsgovernment data in an accessible, user-friendly manner.Specific courses required for project execution: Web Development, API Developmentor related coursework,Description: This project focuses on developing BITS TEC, a dedicated eventmanagement application for BITS Pilani. The app will enable event creation, discovery,RSVP management, real-time notifications, and post-event feedback collection.Students will build mobile-responsive interfaces, implement real-time features usingWebSockets, integrate calendar and notification systems, and develop an admindashboard for event organizers. The project emphasizes user experience, performanceoptimization, and scalable architecture to handle campus-wide events.Skill sets: (i) Proficiency in mobile/web app development (React Native or Flutter,React.js).(ii) Understanding of real-time systems, push notifications, and backend development.Expected learning: Development of a fully functional event management app with real-time features and scalable architecture.Specific courses required for project execution: Mobile App Development, WebDevelopment or related coursework

project_details:
Title: Automation of electronic measurement devices
Description: This project involves designing and implementing a graphical user interface (GUI) for anapplication that performs measurement using vector network analyzer (VNA). The underlyingcomputational and data processing components are developed in Python, and the GUI will serve as aninteractive front-end for visualizing, controlling, and analyzing electromagnetic parameters. The goal isto create a user-friendly, intuitive, and robust interface suitable for use in research or industrial labenvironments exploring AI capabilities to self-serve and insightful analysis.Skill sets:• Proficiency in Python programming• Experience with GUI frameworks such as Tkinter, PyQt, or Kivy• Basic understanding of electromagnetic theory (e.g., wave propagation, materialcharacterization)• Familiarity with data visualization libraries (e.g., Matplotlib, Plotly)• Understanding of software development best practicesExpected learning:• Hands-on experience in designing and developing professional-grade GUI applications inPython• Integration of scientific computation modules with a user interface• Enhanced understanding of electromagnetic concepts through practical application• Development of debugging and testing skills for GUI-based applications• Exposure to user experience (UX) principles and design thinkingSpecific courses required for project execution:• Electromagnetic Fields and Waves / Electromagnetic Theory• Introduction to Python Programming• GUI Programming with Python (or similar elective)

name: NeSL Asset Data Limited (NADL)
project_details:
ATLAS – Anomaly Tracking & Log Analysis System for Distributed Microservices,Proactive Traffic Prediction & Smart Caching for AA Ecosystem
Description: ATLAS is an advanced observability and anomaly detection initiative designed forNADL’s Java Spring Boot based microservices ecosystem, integrated with existing monitoringinfrastructure such as Grafana.Modern distributed systems generate high-volume, high-velocity logs that are often underutilizedbeyond manual debugging. ATLAS aims to transform these logs into structured, analyzableoperational intelligence by:Detect recurring exception patterns across services.Identifying cross-service inconsistencies and abnormal system behavior. Applying statistical and rule-based anomaly detection techniques.Detect abnormal error rate spikes and service degradation trends.Skill sets: Python for ML prototyping, NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn, Basic NLP concepts, Time-series analysis, Probability & statistics, Observability concepts (logs, metrics, traces), Familiaritywith Grafana dashboards.Expected learning:Apply unsupervised and statistical anomaly detection methods on real-world systemdata.Compare rule-based monitoring with ML-based anomaly detection approachesDevelop production-oriented design documentation aligned with industry standards.Specific courses required for project execution: Distributed Systems, Machine Learning, DataMining.,Description: This project focuses on predicting system request load and optimizing data flowin the Account Aggregator framework. By analyzing historical transaction and consent requestlogs, the system predicts upcoming traffic spikes and preloads or caches frequently requestedfinancial data which results in:Reduced API response latency.Improved reliability during peak load.Efficient resource utilization (CPU, memory, DB connections).Better UX for end-users due to faster response times.Instead of waiting for spikes to occur, the system anticipates requests by using time-seriesforecasting (LSTM) to predict request load and prepares the backend in advance, like “smartprefetching” used in high-scale systems.Skill sets: Python for modeling (time-series forecasting), Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, LSTM,RNN concepts, Monitoring & alerting.Expected learning:Learn about time-series forecasting applied to real system loads.Build a predictive caching layer to improve latency.Understand traffic patterns and load balancing in a microservices environment.Gain hands-on experience with Redis caching and proactive resource management.Specific courses required for project execution: Machine Learning, Time-Series Analysis,Performance Engineering, Systems Design.

name: Promark Techsolutionspvt ltd
project_details:
Title: Design and Fabrication of a Sliding Shutter Assembly for 75 Inch IFPD
Description: The project involves the design, development, and fabrication of a precision-engineered sliding shutter mechanism for a 75-inch Interactive Flat Panel Display (IFPD). The system integrates a mobile marker writing panel and a fixed pin-up board, ensuring both protection and functional utility. The work includes CAD-based design, dimensional validation, material and mechanism selection, and fabrication using CNC sheet metal processes. Emphasis is placed on kinematic smoothness, structural rigidity, tolerance control, and long-term operational reliability.Skill sets: Mechanical design fundamentals, engineering drawing interpretation, basic CAD proficiency, sheet metal fabrication processes, measurement and tolerance concepts, assembly and fitment understandingExpected learning: Application of design-for-manufacturability (DFM), exposure to CNC laser cutting and bending processes, understanding of tolerance stack-up and alignment, integration of design with fabrication constraints, hands-on experience in assembly and functional validationSpecific courses required for project execution:  Engineering Mechanics, Mechanics of Solids, Engineering Graphics / Machine Drawing, Computer Aided Design (CAD), Manufacturing Technology / Workshop Practice, and basic exposure to Machine Design and Metrology

Title: Social Media Agentic AI Workflow – Content Creation, Scheduling & Analytics
Description:This project involves designing and deploying an agentic AI workflow that autonomously creates, schedules, and publishes social media content across platforms. The system integrates with social media APIs (Instagram, YouTube, LinkedIn, Twitter/X) to perform data analytics and optimize post reach and engagement. The project also includes the development of a web-based admin dashboard for managing content pipelines, scheduling queues, and analytics dashboards. name: METASRUJAN
project_details:
Title: Image processing using MATLAB/Python
Description: This project aims to develop and implement an Inverse Synthetic Aperture Radar (ISAR)imaging system using MATLAB or Python, focusing on signal processing algorithms and imageformation techniques. ISAR is a powerful radar imaging methodology used to generate high-resolutionimages of moving targets, such as aircraft or ships, by exploiting their relative motion with respect to theradar platform.This work has practical relevance in defense surveillance, remote sensing, target identification, andreconnaissance operations. It also provides hands-on exposure to core concepts in digital signalprocessing, time-frequency analysis, and radar system design.Skill sets:• Strong understanding of radar principles and wave propagation• Proficiency in MATLAB and/or Python for scientific computing• Knowledge of signal processing techniques (e.g., FFT, filtering, windowing)• Familiarity with radar imaging concepts (e.g., range-Doppler, cross-range resolution)• Experience with data visualization and image processing libraries (e.g., matplotlib, OpenCV)• Ability to work with simulated or real radar datasets• Problem-solving and debugging skills in a programming environment• Technical writing and documentation proficiencyExpected learning:• Comprehensive understanding of ISAR image formation theory and techniques• Hands-on experience with radar signal simulation and processing in MATLAB/Python• Ability to implement and optimize algorithms for motion compensation and focusing• Familiarity with real-world radar challenges like noise, clutter, and resolution limits• Insight into advanced signal processing methods used in modern radar systems• Development of software and analytical skills critical to defense and aerospace R&DSpecific courses required for project execution:• Digital Signal Processing – for handling and transforming radar signals• Electromagnetic Wave Theory – for understanding wave interactions and radar returns• Programming for Engineers – for implementing and debugging ISAR algorithms• Pattern Recognition or Image Processing – for target interpretation and analysis• Time-Frequency Analysis – for dynamic target signal characterization• Applied Linear Algebra – for signal transformation and filtering techniques

name: METASRUJAN
project_details:
Design and Optimization of a Wideband Standard Horn Antenna (800 MHz – 18 GHz)
Description: This project is focused on the end-to-end design, simulation, and performance evaluationof a wideband standard horn antenna operating across the 800 MHz to 18 GHz frequency range. Suchultra-wideband (UWB) horn antennas are critical components in broadband measurement systems,EMC testing, radar front ends, and antenna calibration setups.The primary objective is to create a horn antenna with high gain, low VSWR, and stable radiationpatterns across the entire band. The project will explore various horn geometries (pyramidal, conical,and dual-ridged), waveguide transitions, impedance matching techniques, and material considerationsto achieve wideband operation..Skill sets:• Understanding of antenna theory and electromagnetic wave propagation• Proficiency in 3D electromagnetic simulation (e.g., HFSS, CST, FEKO)• Basic knowledge of waveguide theory and transition design• Mechanical design using CAD tools (e.g., SolidWorks, Fusion 360)• Scripting and automation for parametric sweeps (e.g., MATLAB, Python)• Familiarity with RF measurement equipment (e.g., VNA, spectrum analyzer)• Technical documentation and research reportingExpected learning:• Ability to design and optimize horn antennas over wide frequency ranges• Deep understanding of waveguide feeding structures and impedance matching• Proficiency in simulation of complex EM structures and interpretation of S-parameters• Hands-on knowledge of CAD modeling and preparing fabrication-ready designs• Insights into real-world fabrication constraints and RF testing methodologies• Enhanced analytical thinking and multi-domain design capability (EM + mechanical)Specific courses required for project execution:• Antenna Theory and Design – for radiation mechanism and parameter tuning• Microwave Engineering – for transmission line and waveguide concepts• Electromagnetic Field Theory – for understanding wave propagation and boundary conditions• Computer-Aided Design (CAD) – for mechanical modeling of antenna structures• High-Frequency Simulation Techniques – using tools like CST, HFSS, COMSOL• RF & Microwave Measurements – for post-fabrication validation• Numerical Techniques in Electromagnetics – for solving Maxwell’s equations in complexgeometries

name: SMARTSA TECH PRIVATE LIMITED-Online
project_details:
Title: Doubt Solver – Multimodal Talking Agentic AI with WhatsApp Integration & Android App
Description:This project focuses on building a personalized, multimodal agentic AI system capable of resolving student doubts through voice, text, and image inputs. The AI agent contextually understands questions across subjects and responds with step-by-step explanations in a conversational, human-like manner. The system integrates with WhatsApp Business API to enable autonomous doubt resolution directly on the platform students already use. Additionally, a native Android application is developed to provide a seamless mobile experience. The project emphasizes real-time inference, multimodal understanding, and autonomous conversation management without human escalation for standard queries.Skill sets:Multimodal AI & Speech Processing: Handling text, voice, and image-based doubt inputs using LLMs and vision modelsAgentic Conversation Design: Building autonomous, context-aware dialogue agents that maintain session historyWhatsApp Business API Integration: Enabling automated doubt solving through WhatsApp using the Meta Cloud APIAndroid App Development: Building a mobile application for doubt submission, history, and AI interactionPersonalization & Adaptive Response: Tailoring explanations to the user's grade level, learning pace, and prior contextExpected learning:Gaining hands-on experience in multimodal AI system design combining voice, text, and image understandingImplementing WhatsApp API-based autonomous workflows for real-world student interactionDeveloping a production-grade Android application integrated with an AI backendDesigning personalized conversational agents that adapt to individual student needsUnderstanding real-time inference pipelines for low-latency doubt resolutionSpecific courses required for project execution:Natural Language Processing & Conversational AI (for dialogue management and response generation)Computer Vision & Multimodal Learning (for image-based doubt interpretation)Mobile Application Development – Android (for building the student-facing app)Software Engineering & API Integration (for WhatsApp Business API and backend orchestration)

name: SMARTSA TECH PRIVATE LIMITED-Online
project_details:
Title: Academic SME Agentic AI – K12 Mathematics Content Generation & Deployment at Scale
Description:This project involves building an agentic AI system that functions as a Subject Matter Expert (SME) in K12 mathematics, capable of autonomously generating high-quality, curriculum-aligned practice content at scale. The system is designed to produce over 10,000 pages of structured mathematics practice material spanning Grades 1 through 12, covering topics from foundational arithmetic to advanced algebra, geometry, and calculus. The generated content is deployed to a production environment accessible to students and educators. A web-based admin panel is developed for content review, quality control, topic configuration, and deployment management. The project emphasizes prompt engineering, content quality assurance pipelines, and scalable AI deployment.Skill sets:Agentic AI & Prompt Engineering: Designing LLM-based agents with structured prompting for consistent, accurate mathematical content generationCurriculum Design & K12 Mathematics: Understanding grade-wise learning objectives and structuring content accordinglyContent Quality Assurance & Evaluation: Implementing automated and human-in-the-loop pipelines to validate mathematical accuracyProduction Deployment & DevOps: Deploying AI-generated content systems to scalable cloud infrastructureWeb Development (Admin Panel): Building a full-stack interface for content management, review workflows, and deployment controlsExpected learning:Gaining hands-on experience in large-scale AI content generation using agentic workflowsImplementing K12 curriculum-aligned mathematics content pipelines from generation to deploymentDeveloping content quality control systems combining automated validation and structured reviewBuilding admin-facing web tools for managing, filtering, and deploying AI-generated educational materialSpecific courses required for project execution:Natural Language Processing & Large Language Models (for content generation and prompt engineering)Mathematics & Curriculum Studies (for K12-aligned content structuring and accuracy)Web Technologies & Full-Stack Development (for admin panel and content management interface)
